summary: DHH announces Rails 0.13, a major release containing over 225 features and fixes developed over 75 days. The release heavily emphasizes Ajax capabilities alongside migrations, performance improvements, and named routes. He notes the growing user base has helped identify and fix edge cases. The post teases the upcoming Rails 1.0 release, timed for OSCON, and celebrates the project's 7th Slashdotting.
key_points: Rails 0.13 delivers 225+ features and fixes, with Ajax support as the headline capability
The growing Rails community is accelerating bug discovery and edge case fixes
The release maintains backward compatibility despite the massive scope of changes
Rails 1.0 is now the next target, with OSCON as the intended stage for its debut
money_quotes: Rumors of our inability to release ever again was surely false.
We're reaching rapid Maturity on the Scalability with Enterprise goodness scale. Really.
Upgrading will undoubtful force you to increase the sex-appeal of both your application and your code.
That little new one-oh is now slated for laser sharp attention.
Delusions of grandeur has been tuned by 37% with this release.
key_insight: Rails 0.13 represented a pivotal moment where the framework's rapid community growth was translating directly into release quality, setting the stage for the landmark 1.0 release.
